# Jay Trainer

## Overview
Jay Trainer is an independent singer-songwriter from Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania. His music spans folk, acoustic rock, Americana, and singer-songwriter genres. He has released eight records over nearly two decades through his own label, Briar Patch Records.

## Genre
Folk, Acoustic Rock, Americana, Singer-Songwriter

## Location
Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, United States

## Label
Briar Patch Records (independent, self-founded)

## Discography
- A Whisper Of Ruin — Deluxe Edition (2019, album, 15 tracks)
- Lifeline (2023, single, Citizen Cope cover)
- Last Days Of Summer (2023, single, Jay Trainer Band)
- The Southern Winds (2023, single, East Coast Mix)
- Blackout Asylum — Remastered (2021, album, 5 tracks)
- Den Of Thieves (2015, album, 6 tracks)
- Of The Sun (2010, album, 10 tracks)
- The Waking Hours (2006, album, 9 tracks)

## Notable Songs
- Better To Breathe
- The Southern Winds
- Fractured
- Wide Open Eyes
- Lovely In Black
- Of The Sun
- Tiny Chinese

## Website
https://jaytrainer.com

## Social Links
- Bandcamp: https://jaytrainer.bandcamp.com
- YouTube: https://www.youtube.com/c/JayTrainerBand
- Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/jaytrainerband/
- Twitter: https://twitter.com/jaytrainerband
- Facebook: https://www.facebook.com/jaytrainerband

## Description
Jay Trainer writes songs born from deep-seated instinct — stories forged in quiet rooms, beside fires, beneath old trees. His catalogue spans from the raw, unpolished live recordings of The Waking Hours (2006) to the expansive, firelight-recorded A Whisper of Ruin — Deluxe Edition (2019). The music is rooted in Pittsburgh, Pennsylvania, shaped by the rivers, bridges, and hills of western Pennsylvania. Every record is a chapter in a life lived through music.

## Influences
Townes Van Zandt, Chris Whitley, Neil Young, Gregory Alan Isakov, folk tradition, blues, Americana

## Recording Philosophy
Home recording by firelight. Analog warmth and digital clarity. One take when it feels right. The room is always part of the sound.
